description: Provider schedule view (RF20), generalized subscription supporting both clinic and professional ownership (closes RN20), and email notifications via AWS SES on appointment lifecycle events

context.stack: NestJS + TypeScript + MongoDB (Mongoose) + AWS SDK v3 (SES)

prompts.name: install-ses-and-config
prompts.description: Install AWS SES SDK and add SES configuration
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Instruct the user to install: cd apps/api && npm install @aws-sdk/client-ses

Update apps/api/package.json:
- Add @aws-sdk/client-ses ^3.0.0 to dependencies

Update apps/api/src/config/configuration.ts:
- Inside the default export object, add:
  ses: {
    region: process.env.AWS_SES_REGION || 'us-east-1',
    fromEmail: process.env.AWS_SES_FROM_EMAIL || 'no-reply@medicano.app',
    accessKeyId: process.env.AWS_SES_ACCESS_KEY_ID,
    secretAccessKey: process.env.AWS_SES_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY,
  }

Do not change other config sections.

prompts.name: generalize-subscription-schema
prompts.description: Add ownerType and ownerId to Subscription schema, replacing the clinic-only model

prompts.name: subscriptions-service-extensions
prompts.description: Add findByOwner and findActiveOwnerIds methods, keep findByClinicId as deprecated alias
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing file CAREFULLY — preserve all existing methods. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Update apps/api/src/subscriptions/subscriptions.service.ts:

1. Add new method:
   async findByOwner(ownerType: 'clinic' | 'professional', ownerId: string): Promise<SubscriptionDocument | null>
   - Validates ownerId is ObjectId
   - returns this.subscriptionModel.findOne({ ownerType, ownerId: new Types.ObjectId(ownerId) })

2. Add new method:
   async findActiveOwnerIds(ownerType: 'clinic' | 'professional'): Promise<Types.ObjectId[]>
   - const subs = await this.subscriptionModel.find({ ownerType, status: 'active', expiresAt: { $gt: new Date() } }).select('ownerId')
   - return subs.map(s => s.ownerId)

3. Update existing findByClinicId to delegate to findByOwner (backward compatibility):
   async findByClinicId(clinicId: string): Promise<SubscriptionDocument | null>
   - return this.findByOwner('clinic', clinicId)
   - Add JSDoc: /** @deprecated use findByOwner('clinic', clinicId) instead */

4. Update enforceClinicProfessionalLimit to call findByOwner('clinic', clinicId) internally instead of findByClinicId.

5. Update create() method:
   - When called with the new dto containing ownerType and ownerId, save as-is
   - When called legacy with only clinicId, set ownerType='clinic' and ownerId=clinicId before saving

Do NOT remove existing tests dependencies.

prompts.name: search-service-rn20
prompts.description: Filter professionals in search by active subscription, closing RN20
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing file. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The SubscriptionsService now has findActiveOwnerIds (created in prompt 3).

Update apps/api/src/search/search.service.ts:
- Inject SubscriptionsService in the constructor
- In the search() method, REMOVE the existing TODO comment about RN20
- Before querying professionals, fetch active professional IDs:
  - const activeProfIds = await this.subscriptionsService.findActiveOwnerIds('professional')
- When building the professional filter, add: _id: { $in: activeProfIds }
- IMPORTANT: if type === 'clinic', skip this entirely (only filter professionals)
- Document with comment: // RN20: only show professionals with active subscription

Update apps/api/src/search/search.module.ts:
- Import SubscriptionsModule
- Add to imports array

prompts.name: schedule-service
prompts.description: Combined schedule endpoint returning slots + appointments together
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The following already exist — import them:
- AvailabilityService at apps/api/src/availability/availability.service.ts
- AppointmentsService at apps/api/src/appointments/appointments.service.ts
- ProfessionalsService
- ClinicProfessional model (for ownership check)
- Role enum, JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard, Roles, CurrentUser

Create apps/api/src/availability/dto/get-schedule-query.dto.ts:
- class GetScheduleQueryDto:
  - @IsDateString fromDate: string
  - @IsDateString toDate: string

Create apps/api/src/availability/services/schedule.service.ts:
- @Injectable class ScheduleService
- constructor injects:
  - availabilityService: AvailabilityService
  - appointmentsService: AppointmentsService
  - professionalsService: ProfessionalsService
  - @InjectModel('ClinicProfessional') clinicProfessionalModel
  - @InjectModel('User') userModel

- async getProviderSchedule(professionalId: string, query: GetScheduleQueryDto, currentUserId: string): Promise<ScheduleResponse>
  - Validate ObjectId
  - const currentUser = await this.userModel.findById(currentUserId)
  - if (!currentUser) throw NotFoundException
  - const professional = await this.professionalsService.findById(professionalId)
  - Authorization:
    - if currentUser.role === 'professional': verify professional.userId === currentUserId; else ForbiddenException
    - if currentUser.role === 'clinic' or 'attendant': verify ClinicProfessional document exists for clinicId of currentUser and this professionalId; else ForbiddenException
    - if any other role (e.g. patient): ForbiddenException
  - const slots = await this.availabilityService.getAvailableSlots(professionalId, { fromDate: query.fromDate, toDate: query.toDate })
  - const fromDateObj = new Date(query.fromDate)
  - const toDateObj = new Date(query.toDate); toDateObj.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999)
  - const appointments = await this.appointmentsService.findAll({ professionalId, dateFrom: fromDateObj.toISOString(), dateTo: toDateObj.toISOString() })
  - // Note: if findAll signature differs, adapt the call accordingly to pass the date range
  - return { fromDate: query.fromDate, toDate: query.toDate, availableSlots: slots, appointments }

Update apps/api/src/availability/availability.controller.ts:
- Add @Get('schedule/professionals/:professionalId')
  @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)
  @Roles(Role.PROFESSIONAL, Role.CLINIC, Role.ATTENDANT)
  getProviderSchedule(@Param('professionalId', ParseMongoIdPipe) id, @Query() query, @CurrentUser() userId)
  - Calls scheduleService.getProviderSchedule(id, query, userId)

Update apps/api/src/availability/availability.module.ts:
- Provide ScheduleService
- Import ProfessionalsModule, AppointmentsModule (with forwardRef if needed), and add User and ClinicProfessional models via MongooseModule.forFeature

Update packages/types/src/availability.ts:
- export interface IScheduleResponse { fromDate: string; toDate: string; availableSlots: IAvailableSlot[]; appointments: any[] }

prompts.name: notifications-templates
prompts.description: Email templates and the date formatting utility
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. These are PURE functions — no DB, no NestJS dependencies. Create apps/api/src/notifications/utils/format-date.ts: - export function formatDateBR(date: Date): string - Returns date in pt-BR format like 'segunda-feira, 15 de abril de 2026 às 14:30' - Use Intl.DateTimeFormat('pt-BR', { weekday: 'long', day: 'numeric', month: 'long', year: 'numeric', hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit', timeZone: 'America/Sao_Paulo' }) Create apps/api/src/notifications/templates/appointment-created.template.ts: - export interface AppointmentCreatedData { patientName: string; professionalName: string; startAt: Date; durationMinutes: number; recipientType: 'patient' | 'professional' } - export function appointmentCreatedTemplate(data: AppointmentCreatedData): { subject: string; html: string; text: string } - subject = 'Novo agendamento na Medicano' - For recipientType=patient: 'Olá ${patientName}, seu agendamento com ${professionalName} foi registrado para ${formatDateBR(startAt)}.' - For recipientType=professional: 'Olá ${professionalName}, um novo agendamento foi registrado com o paciente ${patientName} para ${formatDateBR(startAt)}.' - html: same content with

tags and on key fields - text: plain text version - Always include a footer: 'Atenciosamente, Equipe Medicano' Create apps/api/src/notifications/templates/appointment-confirmed.template.ts: - export interface AppointmentConfirmedData { patientName: string; professionalName: string; startAt: Date } - export function appointmentConfirmedTemplate(data): { subject; html; text } - subject = 'Seu agendamento foi confirmado' - body: 'Olá ${patientName}, seu agendamento com ${professionalName} para ${formatDateBR(startAt)} foi confirmado.' Create apps/api/src/notifications/templates/appointment-cancelled.template.ts: - export interface AppointmentCancelledData { recipientName: string; otherPartyName: string; startAt: Date; cancelledBy: 'patient' | 'provider' } - export function appointmentCancelledTemplate(data): { subject; html; text } - subject = 'Agendamento cancelado' - If cancelledBy='patient': 'Olá ${recipientName}, o agendamento com ${otherPartyName} para ${formatDateBR(startAt)} foi cancelado pelo paciente.' - If cancelledBy='provider': 'Olá ${recipientName}, o agendamento com ${otherPartyName} para ${formatDateBR(startAt)} foi cancelado pelo prestador.'

prompts.name: notifications-service
prompts.description: AWS SES email sending service with appointment notification methods

prompts.name: wire-notifications-into-appointments
prompts.description: Hook NotificationsService into appointment create/confirm/cancel flows
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing file CAREFULLY — preserve all existing methods and logic. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

The NotificationsService already exists (created in prompt 7).

Update apps/api/src/appointments/appointments.service.ts:
- private readonly logger = new Logger(AppointmentsService.name)
- Inject NotificationsService in the constructor

1. In create() method, after the document is saved:
   - this.notificationsService.notifyAppointmentCreated(appointment).catch(err => this.logger.warn('Notification failed', err.stack))
   - Do this BEFORE the return

2. In createForPatient() method, after the document is saved:
   - Same pattern

3. In updateStatus() method, after appointment.save():
   - if (dto.status === AppointmentStatus.CONFIRMED): notifyAppointmentConfirmed(appointment).catch(...)
   - if (dto.status === AppointmentStatus.CANCELLED): notifyAppointmentCancelled(appointment, 'provider').catch(...)

4. In cancel() method (provider cancellation), after status update:
   - notifyAppointmentCancelled(appointment, 'provider').catch(...)

5. In cancelAsPatient() method, after status update:
   - notifyAppointmentCancelled(appointment, 'patient').catch(...)

CRITICAL: notification calls must NOT use await. They are fire-and-forget with .catch() to ensure failure does not break the main flow.

Update apps/api/src/appointments/appointments.module.ts:
- Import NotificationsModule
- Add to imports array

prompts.name: tests-schedule-and-notifications
prompts.description: Unit tests for ScheduleService and NotificationsService
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Create apps/api/src/availability/tests/schedule.service.spec.ts:
- Mock availabilityService, appointmentsService, professionalsService, clinicProfessionalModel, userModel
- afterEach jest.clearAllMocks()

Tests:
1. 'getProviderSchedule — combined response returns slots and appointments'
   - Mock currentUser as a professional whose userId matches professional.userId
   - Mock availabilityService.getAvailableSlots to return [slot1, slot2]
   - Mock appointmentsService.findAll to return [appt1]
   - Expect return value to include both arrays

2. 'getProviderSchedule — professional accessing own schedule allowed'
3. 'getProviderSchedule — clinic accessing linked professional allowed' (mock ClinicProfessional.findOne to return a doc)
4. 'getProviderSchedule — clinic accessing UNLINKED professional throws ForbiddenException' (ClinicProfessional.findOne returns null)
5. 'getProviderSchedule — professional accessing another professional throws ForbiddenException'
6. 'getProviderSchedule — patient role throws ForbiddenException' (or never reaches service if RolesGuard catches earlier — at the unit level test the service still rejects it)

Create apps/api/src/notifications/tests/notifications.service.spec.ts:
- jest.mock('@aws-sdk/client-ses') with a mock SESClient that has a send method
- const mockSend = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ MessageId: 'fake' })
- Mock patientModel, userModel, professionalsService, configService
- Mock configService.get('ses') to return { region: 'us-east-1', fromEmail: 'no-reply@medicano.app' }

Tests:
1. 'notifyAppointmentCreated — sends email to both patient and professional' (verify mockSend called twice with correct addresses)
2. 'notifyAppointmentConfirmed — sends email only to patient' (1 call)
3. 'notifyAppointmentCancelled — when cancelledBy=patient, sends to professional only'
4. 'notifyAppointmentCancelled — when cancelledBy=provider, sends to patient only'
5. 'sendEmail — SES error is caught and logged, does not throw' (mock send to reject; expect no throw)

prompts.name: tests-subscriptions-and-search-rn20
prompts.description: Extend subscriptions and search tests for the new generalized model and RN20 enforcement
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ the existing files and PRESERVE all existing tests. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

Update apps/api/src/subscriptions/tests/subscriptions.service.spec.ts:
- Add inside the existing describe block:
  1. 'findByOwner(clinic, id) — returns clinic subscription'
  2. 'findByOwner(professional, id) — returns professional subscription'
  3. 'findActiveOwnerIds — filters by status=active and expiresAt > now'
  4. 'findByClinicId — backward compat alias delegates to findByOwner(clinic, id)'

If existing tests break because the schema now requires ownerType/ownerId, update test fixtures to include them.

Update apps/api/src/search/tests/search.service.spec.ts:
- The constructor now injects SubscriptionsService — provide a mock
- Mock subscriptionsService.findActiveOwnerIds('professional') by default to return all professional IDs in the test (so existing tests pass)

Add new tests:
  1. 'search — filters out professionals without active subscription (RN20)'
     - Mock findActiveOwnerIds to return only [profId1] when test data has [profId1, profId2]
     - Verify search result.professionals only contains profId1
  2. 'search — clinics are not affected by professional subscription filter'
     - Mock findActiveOwnerIds to return []
     - Verify result.clinics still contains all clinics matching the filter
